channel.title: Winston Lin
channel.description: Selected case studies, writing, and Python tools across serverless, platform products, business systems, and operator work.

item.title: How I Use AI as a PM With a Real Workspace
item.description: The useful version of AI for product work is a real workspace that keeps notes, data, drafts, and assets in the same loop.

item.title: Growth Breaks In The Operating Model
item.description: A lot of growth problems are really handoff, pricing, and service-definition problems wearing a growth label.

item.title: AI Support Is A Workflow Design Problem
item.description: The hardest part of AI support is usually not the model. It is deciding what the system can do, when it should stop, and how it fits the real workflow.

item.title: Trustworthy AI Products Need Recovery Paths
item.description: Trust comes less from the best answer than from what the system does when it is unsure.

item.title: Developer Platforms Mostly Fail On Friction
item.description: Most platform products lose in the first hour, not in the roadmap review.

item.title: Feature Registries Are About Operational Clarity
item.description: A registry matters when it can answer what broke, who owns it, and what changed while something is already going wrong.

item.title: Migration Readiness Is A Product Problem
item.description: Migration pain is often designed into packaging, docs, and defaults long before a customer ever starts.

item.title: What Building Internal Platforms Taught Me
item.description: Internal platforms teach product judgment quickly because the users are technical, close to the work, and too busy to tolerate fluff.

item.title: Why I Still Like Small Python Tools
item.description: Small scripts still earn their keep when they remove one repeated annoyance from the week.
